heroes.note: JCI plunged 4.2%, rupiah near record-low Rp18,036, foreign net sell Rp3.7T in a single session, MSCI downgrade risk pending June review, and inverted bond yield curve compound severe market stress.

Persistent foreign outflows (YTD net sell Rp54T), rupiah at historic lows, MSCI classification risk on 23 Jun, and BI's surprise 50bps hike in May signal ongoing deleveraging pressure on equities and bonds.

news.title: JCI Crashes 4.2% to 5,594 — Worst Single-Day Drop in Months as Foreign Selling Hits Rp3.72T
news.impact: Systemic de-risking by foreign investors across large-caps accelerates near-term downside, with BBCA -6.45% and BREN -10.25% signalling contagion risk to index heavyweights over the next 1-2 weeks.

news.title: Rupiah Weakens to Rp18,036/USD — Near All-Time Low; BI Intervenes
news.impact: Currency at historic low boosts imported-inflation risks and tightens financial conditions, pressuring rate-sensitive sectors Banking and Property over the next 3-4 weeks.

news.title: MSCI Annual Market Classification Review Due 23 June — Indonesia Downgrade Risk Still Live
news.impact: A potential demotion from Emerging Market status would trigger forced index-fund selling of Indonesian equities estimated in the billions of dollars, representing the single largest structural risk to IDX through end-June.

news.title: Indonesia Parliament Passes New Commodity Export Oversight Legislation — Tightens State Control
news.impact: Heightened state intervention in commodity exports raises cost-of-compliance and regulatory risk for miners and planters, likely weighing on ADRO, PTBA, AALI, and LSIP valuations for 1-3 months.

news.title: OECD Cuts Indonesia 2026 GDP Forecast to 4.7% from 4.8%
news.impact: Downward growth revision adds to bearish sentiment and could prompt further portfolio outflows from EM-dedicated funds, compounding pressure on IDR and equities near-term.

news.title: Danantara Sovereign Wealth Fund Rated Baa2/BBB by Moody's, S&P, Fitch — Moody's Outlook Negative
news.impact: Negative outlook from Moody's signals market concern about fiscal trajectory; however, successful bond issuance at sovereign-parity rating provides some liquidity buffer for state-linked entities over 3-6 months.

news.title: Bank Indonesia Warns Oil Above $100 Has Double-Edged Impact — Export Commodity Boost vs. Inflation Risk
news.impact: Rising coal, CPO, and gold export revenues partially offset external balance deterioration, but elevated oil import costs keep the trade surplus thin and inflation risks elevated over the next 1-2 months.

regulations.title: BI Regulation on SRBI (Rupiah Securities) Optimization
High Shareholding Concentration (HSC) Disclosure Framework — IDX/OJK
regulations.sub: Implemented ahead of MSCI deadline · Apr 2026

calendar.detail: BI-Rate decision — hold or further hike

precedents.html: In May–June 2013, the 'Taper Tantrum' sent the JCI down ~20% in weeks as foreign bond outflows exceeded Rp30T and the rupiah fell past Rp10,000/USD — BI ultimately hiked 175bps to stabilise the currency, an analogy to today's BI emergency hike cycle.

During the 2015 China slowdown shock, the JCI fell from 5,500 to 4,100 (–25%) over six months as commodity prices collapsed and the rupiah weakened to Rp14,700/USD; recovery required a 12-month fiscal consolidation effort before foreign flows returned.

In March 2020, the JCI crashed 37% to 3,937 and the rupiah hit Rp16,600/USD during COVID-19; however, coordinated BI bond-buying of Rp600T+ and global Fed easing drove a full recovery within 12 months, suggesting policy firepower remains a key circuit-breaker.

geo.html: US–Iran conflict near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz drives Brent crude above $95/bbl, inflating Indonesia's oil import bill and narrowing the trade surplus → pressuring the current account → weakening the rupiah → forcing BI rate hikes → compressing equity multiples. Now: Trade surplus collapsed to $0.09B in April; rupiah at record-low Rp18,036.

China energy-security scramble post-Hormuz disruption drives thermal coal imports up 27% YoY → Newcastle coal surges to $147/ton (+40% YoY) → revenue windfall for ADRO, ITMG, PTBA → partial offset to IDX Mining vs. broad-market selloff. Now: Coal sector outperforming broader index but new export-oversight law introduces regulatory overhang.

US Fed maintaining elevated rates → strong USD → capital flight from EM Asia → Indonesia bears disproportionate pressure as MSCI downgrade risk amplifies forced selling by passive global funds → YTD foreign equity outflows reach Rp53.97T, the largest since the 2018 EM crisis. Now: MSCI classification verdict due 23 Jun; outcome is binary and high-stakes for the entire IDX.
